steps.title: Express length in terms of width using the ratio
steps.content: The ratio 10:4 tells us that $\frac{L}{W} = \frac{10}{4} = 2.5$. So length is always 2.5 times the width.

steps.title: Apply the width change to find the new length
steps.content:
When width increases from $W$ to $W + 7$, the new length must maintain the same relationship:
$$L_{\text{new}} = 2.5(W + 7) = 2.5W + 17.5$$
Since the original length is $L = 2.5W$, we have $L_{\text{new}} = L + 17.5$.

The length must increase by 17.5 units. That matches choice C — C is correct.
